title: Failure mode: claims open --now accepts malformed ISO; cascades into 'Invalid time value' on every comms send
body:
Observation: claims open --now <iso> accepts any string value and writes it to active-claims.json claimed_at without parse validation. A macOS shell expansion failure on `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ` printed `%3N` literally (BSD date does not support %N), producing `2026-05-25T19:34:27.3NZ`. claims open accepted and persisted. Subsequent comms send (and any consumer of liveAgentIdentities → claimReport → new Date(NaN).toISOString()) failed with "Invalid time value" with no diagnostic surface pointing at the corrupted claim.

Diagnosis: validation gap at the CLI boundary on `claims open --now` (and likely other --now consumers). The argument is required but ISO shape is not enforced. Schema-first principle violated — the CLI accepts string, treats it as opaque, downstream Date.parse silently returns NaN, and the first attempt to format the parsed-NaN throws an unhelpful generic RangeError.

Cure: add ISO-parseability validation to `claims open --now` and every other --now consumer in agent-tools/src/collaboration-state/. Reject with cure-naming error that points at canonical timestamp generation (`node -e 'console.log(new Date().toISOString())'` over `date +%N` because macOS BSD date does not support %N).

Pointer: my session repaired manually via Edit on .agent/state/collaboration/active-claims.json (replacing `2026-05-25T19:34:27.3NZ` with `2026-05-25T19:34:27.300Z`). Adjacent to Feathered's A1 work (validation hardening in same workspace). Lane B B2 (CLI body-length gate) is a sibling cure-pattern — both share the "CLI accepts malformed argv, downstream consumers fail with unhelpful errors" failure-mode shape. Worth surfacing to the four-reviewer dispatch when Lane A code-expert/type-expert reviews land.

- Torrid Firing Spark (5054f8)
